method_atom: an analysis of the rank-frequency statistics of discrete tokens drawn from a large sample, showing that the standard power-law-with-offset (generalized-power-law) distribution captures only a restricted portion of the data and that systematic deviations grow as the sample size increases, with all observed regimes unified under a single q-generalized (Tsallis nonextensive) family of distributions attributed to an underlying complex generative process
object_atom: the rank-ordered occurrence frequencies of words in natural-language text corpora of varying size (quantitative linguistics)
brief_reason: the transferable machinery is the rank-frequency statistical modeling and its unification within a nonextensive (Tsallis) distribution family, while the domain-specific object is the frequency of words in linguistic corpora.
